title: pelargonium × graphicum
intro: Pelargonium × graphicum, also known as the Rose Geranium, is a hybrid plant species within the Geraniaceae family, characterized by its fragrant, rose-scented leaves and stems. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in South Africa, where it is native, for its medicinal properties, including antimicrobial, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rose Geranium include reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep quality, and alleviating symptoms of menopause, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant's essential oil is commonly used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is also added to skincare products due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
